name: Col des Sept Frères - Roquefeuil Loop
description: This moderate 19.2-mile (30.9 km) road cycling route in Pays de Sault features 2047 feet (624 metres) of elevation gain.
region_intro: Hiking around Comus, located in the Aude department, offers a diverse landscape characterized by its proximity to the Pyrenees foothills. The region features varied terrain, including gorges, valleys, and elevated viewpoints. Trails often traverse forested areas and open country, providing a range of experiences for hikers. Best hiking trails around Comus The most popular hiking route is Comus – Gorges de la Frau loop from Comus, a 3.8 miles (6.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 42 minutes to complete. This easy route explores the scenic Gorges de la Frau. Another top favourite among local hikers is Comus – View of Montaillou loop from Comus, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.5 km) path. This trail offers panoramic views towards Montaillou and traverses varied terrain. Local hikers also love the View of Pic de Rives – Comus loop from Comus, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through elevated landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Comus is defined by its gorges, elevated viewpoints, and varied forest and open country paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Comus are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 230 reviews. More than 1800 hikers have used komoot to explore Comus's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking around Comus?
A: Hiking around Comus, located in the Aude department, offers a diverse landscape. You'll find varied terrain including gorges, valleys, and elevated viewpoints. Trails often traverse forested areas and open country, providing a range of experiences.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Comus?
A: Yes, Comus offers several easy trails. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10534283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comus – Gorges de la Frau loop from Comus&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.8 miles (6.2 km) long and explores the scenic Gorges de la Frau. There are a total of 2 easy routes available.

Q: What are some challenging hikes available in the Comus area?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20532984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges de la Frau – Gorges de la Frau loop from Comus&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult route spans 16.2 miles (26.1 km) with significant elevation changes. Overall, there are 2 difficult routes in the area.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Comus?
A: Many of the trails around Comus are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35332170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comus – View of Montaillou loop from Comus&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15020929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pla du Boum – Comus loop from Comus&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Comus?
A: The trails in Comus are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 230 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, scenic gorges, and elevated viewpoints.

Q: What interesting natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking near Comus?
A: While hiking near Comus, you can explore several notable natural features and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/456262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges de la Frau&lt;/a&gt; are a prominent natural attraction. You might also encounter various mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2725232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pradel Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2491499" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col des Sept Frères&lt;/a&gt;, offering elevated views.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles to visit near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region around Comus is rich in history. You can visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/455335" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montségur Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a significant historical site. There's also a specific viewpoint, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2954133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Montségur Castle&lt;/a&gt;, that offers excellent photographic opportunities.

Q: How many hiking trails are there in total around Comus?
A: There are 14 hiking trails available around Comus, offering a range of distances and difficulties for all types of hikers.

Q: What are some of the best viewpoints accessible from the trails?
A: Many trails in Comus lead to elevated viewpoints. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/35332170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comus – View of Montaillou loop from Comus&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views towards Montaillou, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15020934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pic de Rives – Comus loop from Comus&lt;/a&gt; takes you through elevated landscapes.

Q: What is the typical duration for a moderate hike in Comus?
A: Moderate hikes in Comus vary in length and duration. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15020934" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pic de Rives – Comus loop from Comus&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.6 miles (10.7 km), typically takes about 3 hours and 17 minutes to complete.

Q: Is the area suitable for hikers looking for varied scenery?
A: Absolutely. The hiking routes in Comus are defined by their diverse scenery, including deep gorges, expansive elevated viewpoints, and a mix of dense forest and open country paths. This variety ensures a dynamic hiking experience.
tips: The Croix des 7 Frères is a small, inconspicuous summit and the start of a nice ride in the area.
Refuge situated at 1262m. I don't know if it's possible to use, but it has a small area to camp. No water sources near.
Do not go through the D613, too many cars ... Instead, take the time and go through this picturesque road.

faqs.question: How challenging is the road cycling route through The little Bois de Niave?
faqs.answer: This route is categorized as moderate for racebike cycling. While it's generally a road ride, be prepared for potential unpaved sections that can add to the challenge. Good fitness is necessary, as the route involves significant elevation gain, around 624 meters (2,047 feet).

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 30.9 kilometers (19.2 miles) long. On average, it takes about 1 hour and 48 minutes to complete. However, depending on your pace and stops, it could take up to 5 hours, especially if you encounter challenging unpaved sections or take time to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: You'll primarily be riding on roads suitable for racebikes. However, it's important to note that some segments may be unpaved and challenging for a road bike. Be prepared for varied surfaces and significant climbs.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting spots. You'll cycle near the Col des Sept Frères, and the Niave Forest Refuge. You'll also experience the natural beauty of Le Petit Bois de Niave itself, which is a forest highlight.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for this exact route isn't detailed, but typically, you can find parking in nearby villages or designated areas close to the start of cycling routes in the region. It's advisable to check local maps or community information for the most convenient starting points.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle The little Bois de Niave?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring, summer, and early autumn months when the weather is most favorable for road cycling. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out, especially for mountain passes like the Col des Sept Frères, which can have unpredictable conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Pays de Sault?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads and trails within the Pays de Sault region. However, always respect private property and any local regulations or signage you may encounter.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While this is a racebike route, if you plan to bring a dog (perhaps in a trailer or for a walk nearby), always ensure they are on a leash, especially near roads or in areas where wildlife might be present. Be mindful of the terrain and duration if considering bringing a pet.

faqs.question: Is this a loop route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is designed as a loop, bringing you back to your starting point. This makes it convenient for planning your ride without needing to arrange for transport back.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find yourself on sections of the Route des Sapins, SC 09 : Espezel - Comus, Camí dels bons homes en BTT, Camí dels bons homes - França, and Des Vosges aux Pyrénées, variante, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to refuel or find refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Specific cafes or refreshment stops directly on this route are not detailed. Given the nature of a moderate road cycling route, it's always recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks. You might find options in nearby villages, but plan to be self-sufficient for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: Due to its moderate difficulty, significant elevation gain, and potential for unpaved sections, this route is generally not recommended for beginner road cyclists. It requires good fitness and some experience with varied terrain. Beginners might find it too challenging.
